definition_en: The latest time by which something must be completed
example: The report must be submitted before the deadline on Friday.

definition_en: Time worked beyond the regular working hours
example: He worked overtime to finish the project on time.

definition_en: To leave a job voluntarily
example: She resigned from her position after ten years.

definition_en: The situation when an employee loses their job because it is no longer needed
example: The factory announced 200 redundancies.

definition_en: A formal assessment of an employee's performance
example: She received excellent feedback in her annual appraisal.

definition_en: Working from a location other than a company office
example: Remote work became very common after the pandemic.

definition_en: A move to a higher position with more responsibility
example: He was given a promotion after exceeding his targets.

definition_en: A person you work with, especially in a professional setting
example: My colleagues are very supportive.

definition_en: To discuss something in order to reach an agreement
example: She negotiated a higher salary before accepting the offer.

definition_en: The ability to act and make decisions without being told to
example: Employers value candidates who show initiative.

definition_en: The amount of work assigned to a person
example: His workload has increased significantly this month.

definition_en: To work together with others to achieve something
example: The two departments collaborated on the new campaign.

definition_en: A trial period at the start of a new job
example: New employees are on probation for three months.

definition_en: A document showing the amount of money earned and deductions
example: Check your payslip to see the tax deductions.

definition_en: Working independently for different companies rather than being employed by one
example: She left her company to work freelance as a designer.

definition_en: A state of physical and mental exhaustion caused by excessive work
example: She suffered from burnout after years of long hours.

definition_en: Information about the quality of someone's work or performance
example: The manager gave constructive feedback on the presentation.

definition_en: A written legal agreement between employer and employee
example: Always read your contract carefully before signing.

definition_en: A system in which people are organised by rank or authority
example: The corporate hierarchy determines who makes decisions.

definition_en: An experienced person who guides and supports a less experienced one
example: Having a good mentor can accelerate your career significantly.

definition_en: The rate at which work is done efficiently
example: The new software improved productivity by 30%.

definition_en: The act of formally leaving a job
example: She handed in her resignation after the meeting.
